what_it_does: Captures a live-session braindump for a GPU/serving/loadout rework: the mandate, a measured FP8-4B profile, the mental model, vLLM flags to adopt, existing extension mechanisms, gathered research, a 7-phase build plan, open questions, and the current temporary runtime state. It is explicitly an unconfirmed input to a research wave and the build plan that follows.
observations: Frontmatter marks it as descriptive, unconfirmed, and captured from a live session.
The mandate is to stop hand-guessing resource params and compute them from measured model costs plus live headroom, expressed in Tim's units.

It records a real measured profile for RedHatAI/Qwen3.5-4B-FP8-dynamic: 5.67 GiB weights, ~17.4 KB KV per token, ~34 MiB per-seq Mamba state, a ~0.43 GiB CUDA-graph blind spot, and a ~5.6 GiB co-resident penalty.

It explains the mental model: KV cache as a shelf, gpu_util as a fraction of total card memory, max_model_len as a per-request ceiling, max_num_seqs as a concurrency cap, and idle history as free via prefix caching.
It lists vLLM flags to make first-class company capabilities, with kv-cache-memory-bytes and enforce-eager as the keystone pair.
It points to existing code to extend: ops/cli/gpu.py auto_gpu_util/budget_vram, ops/cli/serveconfig.py, runtime/capabilities, and the loadout/combo system.
It outlines a 7-phase plan from auto-profiler through repo gate-reds, with phases 1-2 as the spine.
It ends with a temporary-state note: chat-4b-fp8 is solo at gpu_util 0.9 / max_num_seqs 32, embedder and TTS are evicted, and resource_capacity T6a is red until restored.
standouts: The 'That 50% has gotta go' mandate: replace hand-tuned gpu_util with computed memory budgets.
The measured 34 MiB per-seq Mamba state versus the architecture-computed ~49.5 MiB, used to justify measuring over computing.
The proposed keystone switch to --kv-cache-memory-bytes plus --enforce-eager for deterministic footprints.
The 7-phase plan and the explicit 'measure manually first, then generalize' process rule.
The current temporary high-utilization config causing an expected test red.
concerns: A single file mixes captured understanding, measured data, mental model, design intent, build plan, open questions, and live operational state, so it may stale quickly.
Status is unconfirmed, yet it cites specific commit hashes and measurements that could drift.
The temporary-state paragraph will become incorrect as soon as the loadout is restored.
Many open questions are listed without clear priority or blocker status.
notes: A live-session capture that grounds a GPU/serving/loadout rework in measured FP8-4B costs, mandates computed budgets over hand-tuned gpu_util, and lays out a 7-phase build plan pending a research wave.
questions: Which files beyond the named modules will change in each phase?
How will the auto-profiler hook into the model-add workflow and the existing _profile schema?
What are Tim's units exactly and where are they encoded?
How is live headroom measured at runtime?
What is the relationship between this scope doc and the output of the referenced /research-wave?
How will loadout selection surfaces be exposed to MCP agents and the RHM?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to capture everything discussed so nothing is lost and to feed a research wave and build plan; the file does that, but it also reads as a preliminary design document and an operational status note.
apparent_themes: Compute resources from real profiles, not heuristics.
Reach for built-in stack capabilities before building custom workarounds.
Treat loadouts as first-class resource contracts.
Make loadout selection visible to Tim, MCP agents, and the RHM.
Use absolute memory budgets and eager execution for deterministic footprints.
Verify by use and commit with no regression.
intention_signals: The file is trying to shift the organization from hand-tuned GPU-fraction serving to a profile-driven, loadout-based serving system where every model's memory costs are measured, budgets are computed in human units, and vLLM/native features are surfaced as company capabilities.
novelty: Distinct in its explicit rejection of gpu_util fraction budgeting in favor of absolute KV-cache bytes plus eager mode, its emphasis on measuring per-seq Mamba state rather than trusting architecture formulas, and its framing of loadouts as multi-actor selectable resource contracts.
contribution: Provides a captured, unconfirmed but detailed starting scope with measured profile data, a mandate, mental model, flag inventory, existing extension points, phased plan, and open questions that a fused system can use to ground subsequent research and build decisions.
summary_for_embedding: Scope document capturing the intent, measured FP8-4B profile, mental model, vLLM flags, existing mechanisms, seven-phase plan, and open questions for a GPU/serving/loadout rework aimed at replacing hand-tuned resource parameters with computed memory budgets.
intention_for_embedding: To preserve a live-session braindump as the input to a research wave and build plan for profile-driven loadout-based serving.
